headline: SpaceX in merger talks with other Musk companies ahead of IPO
summary: STORY: SpaceX is exploring deals with other companies helmed by Elon Musk.The rocket maker is in discussions to merge with artificial intelligence company xAI ahead of a blockbuster public offering planned for this year.The combination would bring Musk’s rockets, Starlink satellites, social media platform X and Grok chatbot under one roof.That's according to a person briefed on the matter and two regulatory filings.SpaceX is also considering a merger with Musk's electric vehicle maker Tesla, according to Bloomberg.The world's richest man is CEO of SpaceX and xAI, which controls X.He also runs Tesla, tunnel firm The Boring Company, and neurotechnology outfit Neuralink.Neither Musk nor any of the firms responded to requests for comment.Shares in Tesla rose 3% in after-hours trade Thursday.Some of its shareholders have long advocated bringing Musk's companies together.Stock Trader Network chief market strategist Dennis Dick says a major risk thesis for Tesla is that "Musk is spreading himself out too much." For SpaceX, a massive deal could complicate its IPO plans.But it might add momentum to efforts to launch data centers into orbit, a key goal for Musk in the escalating AI race. xAI shares would be exchanged for SpaceX shares if the two merged. A Reuters source says no final agreement has been signed and the deal timing and structure remain fluid.

headline: 'Magnificent Seven' earnings: Meta wows, Microsoft and Tesla mixed
summary: STORY: Meta, Microsoft and Tesla kicked off a wave of Big Tech earnings Wednesday, and it was the Facebook parent company that did most to wow investors. Meta saw ad revenues surge and forecast first-quarter revenue above Wall Street expectations. It also boosted capital expenditure plans by 73% in pursuit of "superintelligence", or where machines surpass human performance.The company will spend up to $135 billion, largely on AI infrastructure. Meta shares rose 10% in extended trading following the numbers. Microsoft also reported a big increase in capital expenditure.But growth slowed at its cloud computing unit, damping investor hopes for a big payoff from the massive spending on artificial intelligence.The Windows maker has enjoyed an advantage in the AI race thanks to its early bet on OpenAI.But it faces growing competition from rivals like Google and AI agents such as Anthropic’s Claude Cowork.They threaten both Microsoft’s AI business and its core software products. Shares in the firm dropped over 6% in after-hours trade as investors weighed up the numbers. There was a mixed picture at Tesla too. Elon Musk's company says it will invest $2 billion in his AI outfit, xAI.It also says production of its Cybercab robotaxi remains on track for this year.The news bolsters Musk’s push to reposition Tesla as an AI and robotics company, in a shift that underpins its roughly $1.5 trillion valuation.But flagging sales of EVs saw revenue fall about 3% in 2025, marking the company’s first annual decline.Demand has been hit as rivals roll out newer models and some buyers are put off by Musk's often contentious rhetoric. Tesla shares rose about 1.8% in after‑hours trade.

headline: Buffett Avoided Tesla for Years — But Now His Company Will Power Elon Musk's Semis
summary: Legendary investor Warren Buffett famously passed at investing in Tesla Inc (NASDAQ:TSLA) stock over the years in the investment portfolio of conglomerate Berkshire Hathaway Inc (NYSE:BRK)(NYSE:BRK). While Berkshire still isn't investing in Tesla stock, the company could help validate the upcoming volume production of the Tesla Semi. headline: Samsung’s Chip Profit Soars in Strong Signal for Global AI Rush
summary: Samsung’s strong showing followed better-than-anticipated results from its smaller rival, suggesting that voracious appetite for AI memory is outstripping global supply, creating a windfall for the two industry leaders.  Meta Platforms Inc. and Microsoft Corp. unveiled sharp gains in capital spending when they reported results overnight.  At the same time, that demand has caused shortages of conventional DRAM and NAND used in modern electronics from personal computers to smartphones.

headline: Tesla plans $20 billion capital spending spree in push beyond human-driven cars
summary: Tesla plans to more than double capital spending to a record high of more than $20 billion this year - but little of it will go to its ​traditional business of selling electric vehicles to human drivers.  The company, which last year lost its global EV sales ‌crown to China's BYD, is instead shifting investment to yet-unproven business lines such as fully autonomous vehicles and humanoid robots, based on executive comments on Wednesday's earnings ‌call.  Highlighting the change, CEO Elon Musk said Tesla would end production of its Model X SUV and Model S sedans and instead use the space in its California factory to make humanoid robots.

headline: Solid-State Batteries: The Next Revolution In Electric Vehicle Battery Technology?

Musk told analysts on the earnings call that Tesla's future is autonomous vehicles and that he is ending Model S and Model X production, cutting down its current product line as it bets big on the Cybercab and Optimus robot.  Tesla stock is up 1.8%.

headline: Tesla earnings call: Musk explains 'amazing abundance' mission
summary: Tesla (TSLA) released fourth quarter results after Wednesday's market close, reporting adjusted earnings of $0.50 per share compared to estimates of $0.45 and revenue of $24.90 billion versus estimates of $25.11 billion.In Wednesday's earnings call, Tesla CEO Elon Musk explained the reasoning behind the company's new mission statement: "amazing abundance."Watch the video above to hear more.To watch more expert insights and analysis on the latest market action, check out Yahoo Finance.

headline: The Latest Things to Know About Tesla's Optimus Robot
summary: Musk said on Wednesday evening that Tesla’s humanoid robot, Optimus, was still in the R&D phase.  Tesla also plans to convert its Model S and X assembly capacity in California to a robot assembly line.  Musk also discussed Tesla’s AI chip strategy on the call.

headline: Musk Talks Memory. Some Stocks Could Benefit.
summary: “Memory is a bigger limiter” than AI logic as companies push to develop artificial intelligence and put it to work in devices such as robots, said Elon Musk on his company's earnings conference call. That could move shares of Micron Technology and other producers of memory on Thursday.

headline: Tesla Details Its Robo-Taxi Plans
summary: Tesla’s robo-taxi service can cover 25% to 50% of the U.S. population by the end of the year, pending regulatory approval, according to Musk.  Tesla, however, does plan to launch a robo-taxi service in the first half of 2026 in seven new cities: Dallas, Houston, Phoenix, Miami, Orlando, Tampa, and Las Vegas.  The populations of those metro areas, plus Austin and San Francisco, where Tesla currently operates robo-taxis, total about 40 million, or about 11% of the U.S. population.

headline: Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) Misses Q4 CY2025 Sales Expectations
summary: Electric vehicle pioneer Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) missed Wall Street’s revenue expectations in Q4 CY2025, with sales falling 3.1% year on year to $24.9 billion. Its non-GAAP profit of $0.50 per share was 10.8% above analysts’ consensus estimates.

summary: Building an AI future will be expensive. Tesla is planning to spend $20 billion on new plants and equipment in 2026, according to CFO Vaibhav Taneja. That is up from about $8.5 billion in 2025. The 2026 guidance is about $10 billion above the Street's expectations.
